failures.message: slide 1 copy contains banned phrase "delve into" — replace with a concrete number, a contrarian claim, or a first-person specific observation
slide 8 copy contains engagement bait "Comment YES" — 2024+ ranker demotes posts whose comments are transactional; the Depth Score formula weights Comment Quality 15× so bait earns reach penalties
slide 8 copy contains engagement bait "Smash that like button" — drop it and replace with a genuine 5+ word question
slide 4 copy contains https://evil.com/solopreneur-stack — links belong in the first comment, not on slides (link-in-comment discipline)
slide 1 image_prompt does not contain slide.copy verbatim — D9 text-baked-in rule violated; the image generator will render different typography than the copy field specifies
slide 3 image_prompt paraphrases the copy instead of quoting it verbatim — D9 text-baked-in rule violated; the compositor needs the exact copy string in the prompt
cover slide copy contains no digit, timeframe, contrarian marker, or curiosity-gap signal — the cover headline will not earn the scroll-stop
failures.evidence: Let me delve into the AI solopreneur stack and tell you everything I think.

failures.message: deck has zero slides with layout_hint='human_fingerprint' — the authenticity slide (typically slides 3-5 per Build in Public structure) is the signal the LinkedIn ranker rewards

slide 1 image_prompt is missing mobile-safe-zone specs (top 150px, bottom 200px, 75px margin, 24pt body minimum, 1080x1350 canvas) — the compositor needs these to avoid the LinkedIn profile overlay and text-cutoff zones
CTA slide copy contains no question mark — without a 5+ word question the slide misses the reply prompt that drives Comment Quality (15× weight in the Depth Score formula)
failures.evidence: Comment YES if you agree with this take on the solopreneur stack. Smash that like button.

failures.message: deck has zero slides with layout_hint='direct_answer' and non-empty direct_answer_block — the 30-80 word AI-search-scrapable summary is missing

suggestions.suggestion: Replace slide 1's opener "Let me delve into" with a concrete metric or contrarian claim — e.g. "12 client projects in 90 days taught me one thing about the solopreneur stack".
Delete "Comment YES if you agree" from slide 8 and replace with a genuine 5+ word question that invites a substantive reply, e.g. "Which layer is breaking first in your stack right now?"
Delete "Smash that like button" from slide 8 — like-baiting phrases are algorithm-demoted; let the content earn the like on its own merits.
Remove https://evil.com/solopreneur-stack from slide 4's copy. LinkedIn applies a ~60% reach penalty to posts with body/slide links — move the URL into the first comment of the post instead.
Rewrite slide 1's image_prompt to include the slide copy verbatim inside double quotes — the image generator must render that exact string as typography (D9 text-baked-in rule).
Rewrite slide 3's image_prompt to quote the full slide copy verbatim instead of paraphrasing "a quote about reasoning". The compositor needs the literal string to render.
Rework the cover to include a number, a timeframe, or a contrarian marker in the first line — e.g. "The 4-layer stack 82% of solopreneurs skip" or "I tested 30 tools. Only 4 layers matter."

Convert one of the body slides (typically slide 3, 4, or 5) into layout_hint='human_fingerprint' with a first-person war-story moment — specific client name or failure mode — to earn the authenticity signal.
Expand slide 1's image_prompt to specify: 1080x1350 canvas, 75px left/right margin, 150px top dead zone (profile overlay), 200px bottom dead zone, 24pt body minimum (or the pixel equivalent).

Rewrite slide 8's copy to end with a 5+ word question that invites a substantive reply — e.g. "Which of these four layers is breaking first for you right now?" Follow the question with the comment sign-off.
Add one slide with layout_hint='direct_answer' and a 30-80 word direct_answer_block that summarizes the deck's thesis for AI search scrapers (Perplexity, ChatGPT search).
